notes: Workflow-tool fan-out. Asserts the user-observable arc: parent ready→working when the workflow starts; each of the N workflow children appears as a session with ParentSessionID = parent.session_id (a subagent under <parent>/subagents/workflows/<run-id>/agent-*.jsonl, never a top-level session); the run's journal.jsonl never surfaces as a session; children transition working→ready as they finish; parent transitions working→ready only after the last child ends; the children are then swept (transcript_removed), returning SubagentCount(parent) to 0. Requires a daemon built from branch fix/565-workflow-subagents (commit 76f9a576) — the pre-fix detector surfaced workflow agents as top-level sessions and journal.jsonl as a phantom 'journal'/'unknown' session.

text: User opt-in prompt ('run a workflow ...') drives parent ready → working as the workflow starts
Workflow child transcripts materialize under <parent>/subagents/workflows/<run-id>/agent-*.jsonl — fswatcher discovers a new SessionState per child

Detector binds child.session_id → parent.session_id (acceptance: ParentSessionID = parent.session_id; children are subagents of the parent, not top-level sessions). SubagentCount(parent) tracks the live child count (peaks at ~3) while children run.

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the workflow children run — the outstanding Workflow tool_use blocks IsAgentDone. The run's journal.jsonl never surfaces as a session (no 'journal' session, no 'unknown'-project session); it is dropped before any lifecycle recording (isWorkflowBookkeepingFile).
Parent transitions working → ready only after the last child ends; children have transitioned working → ready as they finished
The workflow children are swept like ordinary child sessions (transcript_removed), returning SubagentCount(parent) to 0 — no stale subagent badge
